finding_results.notes: Added sensory/bodily and social-impact consequence lines alongside every flagged procedural span so administrative detail no longer displaces scene presence.
Removed suspended-Lena transfer action. Evidence now moves only after explicit on-duty custodian designation, witness capture, and timestamped chain logging.
Inserted repeated Lena interior markers (somatic response, evaluative thought, memory pressure) across the New Hope opening to maintain close-third anchoring and break external-only run length.
Added explicit Ava-centered interior beats before the hall scene and near the final evidence-monitor sequence, linking Ava to Lena's personal obligation rather than procedure alone.

summary: All pass-scoped structural/craft findings were resolved: chain-of-custody causality is now legally coherent on-page, Lena's focal interiority is sustained through the hall section, and Ava's death carries concrete emotional and bodily consequence at both opening and ending beats.
